plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-280 RP08.P02.M07.S13-RP08.P02.M09.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-280 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP08.P02.M07 test/golden tail (validation error mapping through integration smoke case), the full M08 Hermes evidence packet service cycle (22 rows), and the M09 Claude review packet head (8 rows) across three sections over 40 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-281 handoff, without opening email/Office-native/sync/filing runtime, rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, idempotency persistence, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-280 (Risk B, 40 units, RP08.P02.M07.S13-RP08.P02.M09.S08, golden_hermes_slice) passes independent read-only review. Coverage reconciles to 40 across deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title distributions and matches the registry requirements, closeout-pack-plan pack, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and review baseline. The implementation (service.js createEmailDmsCoreCp280GoldenHermesSlice* family) produces descriptor-only case sets with all email/office-native/sync/filing dispatch, DB/object-storage writes, lock acquisition, idempotency/workflow persistence, state-transition writes, rollback/retry runtime, command runtime, and Hermes runtime-receipt flags set false, and synthetic_only/no_real_data/customer_safe_errors_only true. validators.js enforces descriptor-only rows plus per-section security guards (no cross-tenant access, deny-over-allow, no audit-hint/validation-error/blocked-claim/lock-token/permission-decision leakage) wherever the relevant row exists; required rows are structurally guaranteed present so guards are effective. The authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and the no-write attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor/evidence/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The contract validator deep-equals the embedded golden_hermes_slice_descriptor, cp280_requirements, and cp280_no_write_attestation against the code-generated objects, providing strong drift protection; historical bindings tail and current_pack are CP00-280. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (exit 0), summary-only review correctly prohibited (Risk B with security/audit and review-evidence deliverables), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools, and the forbidden_review_evidence list (auth failures, zero-byte/malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields) is complete. The pack does not assert production_ready=true: command-evidence and manifest report production_ready=false / status implemented_pending_review; the descriptor-verified flag and the Hermes production_ready_candidate (gated on coverage.valid AND slice.valid) are appropriately scoped and not an enterprise-trust overreach. All pre-review validation commands are recorded as exit 0, with closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred to the post-review final-closeout step per the hardened sequence. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the eight pack files and excludes unrelated dirty files (LDIP result JSON, the v2.0 .docx, and the handoff note). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; this is a clean continuation of the established CP272-CP279 descriptor-only pattern. Note that Claude is a read-only reviewer and not the final approver — human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim and for opening runtime in downstream packs.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-280 email dms core golden hermes slice descriptor pack
